title: Heat-related Illnesses
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Heat rash is skin irritation caused by excessive sweating during hot, humid weather.
Heat rash symptoms include small red bumps, usually on the neck, upper chest, groin, under the breasts, or elbow creases.
First aid includes working in a cooler and less humid environment and keeping the rash area dry.
Heat illness symptoms such as heat cramps, heat exhaustion, and heat stroke require separate handling.
